text:
Epilepsy Centre 

Neurosciences Division 

Clinic Date: 30 December 2024 

Dr Jonathan Reid  
St George's University Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust  
Neurology Outpatients, Blackshaw Road, London SW17 0QT  

Dear Dr Jonathan Reid,  

Emily Carter, DOB: 14-02-1992, Hospital No: H458219 NHS No. 9081763421  
12 Brookside Close, London, SW12 0AB  

Diagnoses: 

Generalised epilepsy. History of migraine (episodic, without aura). 

I reviewed Emily Carter in the epilepsy clinic today. She attended with her partner. Since our last contact she reports improved recognition of early seizure symptoms and has kept a detailed monthly diary. Although her background includes migraine, these have been infrequent over the past two months and the current clinical focus remains seizure control.

Seizure profile and frequency: 
- Focal seizure: 2024: Feb x0, Mar x1, Apr x0, May x1, Jun x1, Jul x0, Aug x0, Sep x2, Oct x1, Nov x1, Dec x0. 

She describes brief focal aware episodes characterised by a rising epigastric sensation with transient word-finding difficulty, lasting under one minute, without loss of awareness and with full recall. There have been no secondary generalised convulsions this year. No injuries reported, and no emergency medication use. Known triggers include sleep deprivation and missed meals; she has made lifestyle adjustments which may account for lower frequency in the latter part of the year.

Medication: She remains on levetiracetam 1 g twice daily and lamotrigine 150 mg twice daily, with good adherence and no reported adverse effects beyond mild irritability early in the course, now settled. No recent changes in prescription, and no use of rescue therapy in 2024.

Investigations and monitoring: Prior MRI (2022) was unremarkable. Routine EEG (2023) showed generalised spike–wave discharges consistent with her diagnosis of generalised epilepsy. Bloods from September 2024 (U&E, LFT, FBC) were within reference ranges. No safety concerns identified today. 

Functioning and safety: She is currently not driving and understands DVLA guidance. She continues to use a seizure diary and has a workplace safety plan in place. Sleep hygiene and regular meals have been reinforced. 

Plan: 
- Continue current anti-seizure medications unchanged. 
- Maintain seizure and headache diary to track any relation between migraines and focal events. 
- Provide patient information on sick day rules and adherence strategies. 
- Routine blood monitoring in 6 months or earlier if clinical change. 
- Follow-up in 6 months, with earlier review if frequency increases or if any generalised convulsive seizures occur. 

Please do not hesitate to contact me if further information is required. 

Yours sincerely

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter provides a monthly count of focal seizures for 2024: Feb 0, Mar 1, Apr 0, May 1, Jun 1, Jul 0, Aug 0, Sep 2, Oct 1, Nov 1, Dec 0. Counting seizures from Feb through Dec gives: 0+1+0+1+1+0+0+2+1+1+0 = 7 seizures over 11 months. January is not mentioned, so we should not assume a value. The highest explicit frequency dimension provided is the aggregate over these months. There are no other seizure types this year. Therefore, normalize as 7 per 11 month.
